steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the main verb for the subject "Amplifying the voices and experiences of Black soldiers." Two rules apply: (1) a gerund phrase acting as subject is grammatically singular, so the verb must be singular; (2) the sentence describes Chester's past work during the Civil War, so past tense is required.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'were': Plural past. A gerund phrase subject requires a singular verb, so "were" fails the number agreement rule. ✗
  • Choice B — 'have been': Plural present perfect. Fails on both counts — plural number and present-perfect tense do not fit the historical past-tense context of the Civil War. ✗
  • Choice C — 'are': Plural present. Fails on both counts — plural number and present tense contradict the past-tense narration of Chester's Civil War work. ✗
  • Choice D — 'was': Singular past. Matches the singular gerund subject and the historical past-tense context of the passage. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
